chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male presented with recurrent chest pain.
history: The patient had a history of ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) in the inferior leads. He underwent coronary angiography (CAG) which revealed only moderate stenosis of the right coronary artery (RCA).
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm. Physical examination revealed no significant abnormalities.
labs: ECG: ST-segment elevation in the inferior leads. OCT: confirmed the diagnosis of SCAD-induced myocardial infarction.
correct_diagnosis: Spontaneous coronary artery dissection (SCAD) of the right coronary artery (RCA)
differentials: Spontaneous coronary artery dissection (SCAD) of the right coronary artery (RCA)
Acute myocardial infarction due to atherosclerotic coronary artery disease
Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with esophageal spasm
correct_ranking: Spontaneous coronary artery dissection (SCAD) of the right coronary artery (RCA)
Acute myocardial infarction due to atherosclerotic coronary artery disease
Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with esophageal spasm
